title_lines: GILGIT-BALTISTAN ELECTION 2026 — REVISION 4.0 PREDICTION SUMMARY (29 May 2026)
ADOPTED THE INDEPENDENT SURVEY 2026 SINGLE-PAGE REPORT AS PRIMARY INPUT. PPP DOMINANT BLOC AT 12. MWM AND ITP ENTER AS STANDALONE SHIA BLOCS. IPP RETAINS THREE SEATS.
Election Date: 7 June 2026 (GBA-24 polling separately scheduled per ECGB notification).

party_projection.driver: Resurgence across Gilgit, Nagar, Hunza, Baltistan, Astore, Ghizer and Ghanche. Deep organisational network, Bilawal-Aseefa roadshow impact, and the consolidation of Shia and progressive votes.
Targeted strongholds in Gilgit-III, Skardu-I and Ghizer-II. Federal alignment advantage in Sunni-leaning urban seats.
Shia mobilisation in Nagar-II and Skardu-II. Maisam Kazim (GBA-8) is the bloc's appointed Chief Minister nominee.
Incumbent CM Gulbar Khan retains Diamer-IV; party flips in Astore-II and Diamer-II as PML-N candidates switch to IPP.
Islami Tehreek Pakistan Shia religious network flips Skardu-IV and Skardu-V.
Diamer-I personal vote and Ghanche-II open-seat fragmentation.

critical_flips.reason: Dr. Muhammad Iqbal flips Gilgit-III for PML-N versus the Rev 3.0 projection of an Aftab Haider PPP win. MWM runner-up indicates Shia bloc presence.
Riaz Akber wins for MWM as a standalone bloc; the Rev 3.0 PTI-backed proxy framing is retired.
Fida M Nashad takes Skardu-III for PPP. The Rev 3.0 JUI-F projection (Wazir Saleem) collapses; JUI-F now wins zero seats.
ITP enters the Assembly through Wazir Ejaz Hussain's Shia religious-network mobilisation.
ITP picks up a second Skardu seat. Survey reports Amjad Zadi as the winner.
Shamsul Haq Lone holds Astore-II but switches from PML-N to IPP. Marks IPP's first general-seat win in this revision.
Atiqullah delivers Diamer-II to IPP. Confirms IPP's three-seat foothold.
Muhammad Naseem consolidates Diamer-III for PPP. JUI-F third indicates residual religious vote.
Outgoing CM Gulbar Khan retains Diamer-IV for IPP. Rev 3.0 had predicted a PML-N flip; survey reverses that call.
Abdul Jahan delivers Ghizer-II to PML-N. PTI proxy vote concentrates at third position only.
Muhammad Ismail flips Ghanche-III to PPP. GBA-24 polling date subject to ECGB revision.

government_formation_scenarios.label: PPP-Led Coalition (70 percent)
government_formation_scenarios.description: PPP (12) alone is one seat short of a simple majority of 13. A PPP + MWM (2) bloc reaches 14 cleanly; adding either ITP (2) or any Independent (2) widens the cushion. Most likely Chief Minister: PPP.

government_formation_scenarios.label: Cross-Bench Coalition (20 percent)
government_formation_scenarios.description: PML-N (3) + IPP (3) + ITP (2) + Independents (2) = 10 seats. Falls short of a majority but could combine with MWM or split PPP loyalists in a horse-trading round to challenge the PPP-led formation.

government_formation_scenarios.label: Hung Assembly (10 percent)
government_formation_scenarios.description: No coalition consolidates cleanly. Reserved-seat allocation and post-poll defections become decisive. CM selection deferred to the federal arbitration round.

gba24_delay_note: GBA-24 (Ghanche-III) polling note: ECGB scheduling subject to revision.
